scope_determination: docs/dev/ticket-20260526-052559.md is a BA spec/ticket document, not a command or agent .md file. It is outside prompt-inspector scope (~/.claude/commands/*.md and ~/.claude/agents/*.md). In --changed-files mode, the scan is restricted to listed files. No command or agent prompt files appear in the changed-files set.

skipped_files.reason: Out of prompt-inspector scope. Prompt-inspector detects verbose non-functional content in ~/.claude/commands/*.md and ~/.claude/agents/*.md only. This file is a BA spec/ticket document (docs/dev/). The change (line 120 regex string replacement in the Edge Cases section) is documentation content in a ticket file — not a command or agent prompt. No verbosity detection rules apply.

summary.estimated_reduction: N/A — no command/agent prompt files in changed-files set
summary.blocking_rationale: Changed file docs/dev/ticket-20260526-052559.md is out of prompt-inspector scope. Zero findings emitted. Per AC-12.1 Mechanism (ii) tagging contract and default-safe rule: no introduced_in_diff: true findings exist. This diff introduces no prompt verbosity violations.

default_safe_acknowledgment: In --changed-files mode, this inspector emits findings only for listed files. An absent/null/untagged finding is NOT interpreted as 'all NEW'. All findings must carry introduced_in_diff: bool per Mechanism (ii). No findings are emitted here because the single changed file is out of scope.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 9 feedback items. The primary finding (C1) confirmed that in --changed-files mode, the scan must be restricted to listed files only; the changed file (docs/dev/ticket-20260526-052559.md) is not a command or agent .md file and is fully out of scope, so the findings list must be empty. C2 confirmed that draft findings #3-#7 were below the 50-line minor threshold and should not be emitted as standalone violations. C3-C5 identified false positives in the draft full-repo advisory list. C6-C7 noted missed material verbosity in commands/dev.md and commands/dev-command.md for a true full-repo advisory scan (not applicable here). C8 confirmed pre-existing violations must not block close. C9 was observation-only.

findings.issue: C1: In --changed-files mode, the scan must be filtered to listed files only. The changed file docs/dev/ticket-20260526-052559.md is not under commands/*.md or agents/*.md. The full-repo advisory findings drafted should not be emitted for this --changed-files run.
findings.proposed_fix: Replace findings list with [] and emit a skipped_files entry for docs/dev/ticket-20260526-052559.md with reason 'out of prompt-inspector scope'.
findings.rationale: Applied. The changed file is a BA ticket document. --changed-files mode restricts the scan to listed files. docs/dev/ticket-20260526-052559.md is not a command or agent .md file. Zero findings emitted.

findings.issue: C2: Draft findings #3, #4, #5, #6, #7 are below the 50-line minor threshold and should not be emitted as standalone violations solely because they match critical-pattern section names.
findings.proposed_fix: Apply severity logic: critical >= 200 lines or >= 30%; major >= 100 lines or >= 15%; minor >= 50 lines; else no finding. Suppress #5, #6, #7 (6, 10, 25 lines respectively). Suppress #3, #4 (23, 25 lines) unless additional verbose sections are found in the same file.
findings.rationale: Applied. All sub-threshold findings suppressed. In this --changed-files run there are zero in-scope files anyway, so the threshold fix is academic for this report.

findings.issue: C3: ## Quality Standards Enforcement in commands/dev-command.md:936-958 and commands/dev.md:1296-1315 contains procedural responsibility routing. Both are below 50-line threshold.
findings.proposed_fix: Do not emit as standalone findings. If retained in file-level verbosity, recommend: 'Deduplicate cross-agent responsibility summaries; preserve as a one-line pointer to agents/dev.md and agents/qa.md.'
findings.rationale: Applied. These sections contain genuine procedural routing (not just fluff) and are below threshold. Not emitted as findings.

findings.issue: C4: agents/ba.md:1420-1439 and agents/rule-inspector.md:156-163 are substantive output/quality checklists for the agent role, not command-doc philosophy sections. Both are below threshold.
findings.proposed_fix: Remove findings #6 and #7. Optional: rename headings to ## Output Checklist / ## Discovery Criteria rather than counting as verbose_lines.
findings.rationale: Applied. ba.md Quality Standards is a pre-submission checklist covering 18 specific procedural items (BDD criteria, complexity tier, setup fields, etc.) — substantive procedural rules. rule-inspector.md Quality Standards is a 5-point methodology guide. Neither qualifies as verbose fluff.

findings.issue: C5: commands/dev-overnight.md:56-108 ## Overview is a 21-step execution flow DAG for an autonomous orchestration loop, not frontmatter-redundant prose. Counting all 56 lines as fluff is overbroad.
findings.proposed_fix: Do not count as verbose unless inspector first determines it lacks executable step references. Recommend compressing/renaming to ## Workflow DAG rather than removing.
findings.rationale: Applied. The Overview section is an ASCII flow diagram of the entire 21-step overnight orchestration pipeline — it is an execution reference map, not explanatory prose. The 'rules not stories' principle targets narrative fluff; a flow DAG is a rule-like artifact.

findings.issue: C6 (OBSERVATION_ONLY): commands/dev.md has larger verbose tutorial/example sections (Agent Development Use Cases lines 1319-1451, Example End-to-End Workflow lines 1452-1493) that dominate severity in a full-repo advisory scan. Expected severity: critical (243 verbose lines).
findings.proposed_fix: In a separate non-changed-files full-repo run, recompute commands/dev.md as a file-level finding including those sections.
findings.rationale: Logged as advisory. This information is consistent with existing prompt-inspector reports (e.g., prompt-inspector-report-20260526-053746.json which already documents this finding with introduced_in_diff: false). Out of scope for this --changed-files run.

findings.issue: C7 (OBSERVATION_ONLY): commands/dev-command.md:961-1109 contains the same tutorial/example pattern (Agent Development Use Cases + Example End-to-End Workflow) and should dominate severity in a full-repo advisory scan.
findings.proposed_fix: In a separate non-changed-files run, include these sections; expected severity is at least major by line count.
findings.rationale: Logged as advisory. Consistent with prior style-inspector reports on dev-command.md. Out of scope for this --changed-files run.

findings.issue: C8: Pre-existing violations must not elevate severity or block close. All command/agent sections outside the current diff must be tagged introduced_in_diff: false.
findings.proposed_fix: Tag all advisory findings introduced_in_diff: false and blocking_for_close: false.
findings.rationale: Applied. All findings in this report carry introduced_in_diff: false (none emitted here since the changed file is out of scope). The summary explicitly sets blocking_for_close: false.

findings.issue: C9 (OBSERVATION_ONLY): commands/clean.md minor severity (72 verbose lines, 9.5%) is plausible for a default full-repo scan but is out of scope for this --changed-files run.
findings.rationale: Logged. Consistent with prior reports (e.g., prompt-inspector-report-20260517-172600.json: 77 verbose lines, 10.1% minor). Out of scope here.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Applied C1 (scope restriction: changed file out of scope, zero findings emitted), C2 (threshold enforcement: sub-50-line findings suppressed), C3 (QSE sections not standalone findings), C4 (ba.md and rule-inspector.md Quality Standards are substantive checklists, not fluff), C5 (dev-overnight.md Overview is a workflow DAG, not fluff), C8 (introduced_in_diff and blocking_for_close tagging applied). Logged C6, C7, C9 as observation_only advisory notes.
